settings.rtp.desc: Monitor file system changes and alert on suspicious activity

dashboard.title: Security Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: System status and real-time protection overview
dashboard.healthWeakAreas: {count} area(s) need attention — click for details.
dashboard.warningsDesc: Review or ignore warnings you have accepted.
dashboard.warn.rtpDisabled.title: Real-time protection is disabled
dashboard.warn.rtpDisabled.detail: Real-time protection monitors file system changes and alerts on suspicious activity. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.folderWatchDisabled.title: Folder watch is disabled
dashboard.warn.folderWatchDisabled.detai: Folder watch automatically scans new/changed files in Downloads, Temp, and Startup folders. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.networkAlertsDisabled.det: Suspicious network alerts show a toast and audit alert when a connection hits a blocklisted IP. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.networkTrafficHistoryDisa: Network traffic history records bandwidth samples for the history chart. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.autoReportsDisabled.title: Auto-generate reports is disabled
dashboard.warn.autoReportsDisabled.detai: Auto-generate reports automatically creates a security report after each scan completes. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.scanHistoryDisabled.title: Scan history is disabled
dashboard.warn.scanHistoryDisabled.detai: Scan history keeps a record of all past scan results. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.externalLookupsDisabled.d: External lookups allow breach checks using HIBP and XposedOrNot. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.geoLookupDisabled.title: Geolocation heat map is disabled
dashboard.warn.geoLookupDisabled.detail: Geolocation heat map resolves IP addresses to display a world map of active connections. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.perimeterMapDisabled.deta: Network perimeter map shows the live connection visualization on the Firewall page. Enable it in Settings → Feature Toggles.
dashboard.warn.definitionsOutdated.title: ClamAV definitions are outdated
dashboard.warn.definitionsOutdated.detai: ClamAV definitions are outdated. Update them from the Malware Scan page.
dashboard.warn.firewallDisabled.title: Windows Firewall is disabled
dashboard.warn.firewallDisabled.detail: Windows Firewall is disabled for one or more profiles. Enable it from the Firewall Management page.
dashboard.warn.highMemory.title: High memory usage detected
dashboard.warn.highMemory.detail: System memory usage is above 90%. Consider closing unused applications.
dashboard.warn.highCpu.title: High CPU usage detected
dashboard.warn.highCpu.detail: System CPU usage is above 90%. Consider closing resource-intensive applications.
dashboard.warn.lowDisk.title: Low disk space
dashboard.warn.lowDisk.detail: Available disk space is below 10%. Consider freeing up space.

scanner.defsMissing: Definitions are missing. They will be downloaded automatically before scanning.

Turn OFF the {profile} firewall profile?

This disables Windows Firewall protection for this network profile until it's turned back on.
firewall.perimeterSummary: {total} total connections · {filtered} match filters · {shown} on map
firewall.searchPlaceholder: Search by process, IP, or hostname…
firewall.noConnectionsMatch: No connections match the current search/filters.
firewall.ruleSearchPlaceholder: Search by name, app, or address…
firewall.importPrompt:
If an imported rule already exists, choose conflict handling:
skip / overwrite / rename
firewall.perimeterDesc1: Click any dot on the map, or a row in the table below, to see its details and actions here.
firewall.perimeterDesc2: {unverified} — not on a known-safe list yet. Most ordinary connections start out this way.
firewall.perimeterDesc4: — hover any term shown in a connection's details for a description.
firewall.detailBandwidthIpv6: Per-connection bandwidth measurement only supports IPv4 TCP connections right now — this one's IPv6 or another protocol. See the Network Monitor page for interface-level throughput instead.
firewall.detailBandwidthState: Bandwidth can only be measured on an actively-open connection — this one is {state}, not ESTABLISHED, so there's no live data flow left to sample.
firewall.ruleCreated: Rule created. It will apply to new connections.
firewall.processNotFound: Process not found (it may have exited).
firewall.processPathUnavailable: Path not available from Process Inspector for this process.
firewall.perimeterDisabled: Network perimeter map is disabled. Enable it in
firewall.bandwidthTooltip: Takes ~2 seconds — Windows samples this connection's live throughput.
firewall.noProcessPath: Couldn't determine the executable path for this process, so it can't be blocked by path.
network.title: Network Monitor
network.subtitle: Active connections and interface bandwidth
network.heatmapCounts: {total} total connection(s) · {filtered} match current filters · {mapped} mapped
network.heatmapNoMatches: No connections match your current filters.
network.historyEmpty: No historical samples yet — samples are recorded every 30 seconds while the app runs.
network.alertsNone: No blocklisted connections detected this session.
passwords.title: Credential Safety Hub
passwords.subtitle: Generate strong passwords and check credentials against breach data
passwords.externalDisabled: External lookups are disabled. Enable them in Settings.
passwords.pwned: This password appears {count} time(s) in known breaches. Do not use it.
passwords.notPwned: This password was not found in HIBP Pwned Passwords.
passwords.noBreaches: No breaches returned for this email.
passwords.hibpNote: Uses HIBP k-anonymity: only the first 5 SHA-1 hash characters are sent. Disable in Settings for offline-only use.
passwords.xposedNote: Uses the XposedOrNot email breach API. Disable in Settings for offline-only use.
reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Browse scan and security reports
reports.chooseReport: Choose a report from the list to view its details without leaving the app.
toast.definitionsUpdatedDetail: ClamAV signatures are up to date.
firewall.glossary.inbound: Someone or something out on the internet connected in to a service running on this PC.
firewall.glossary.outbound: This PC (or an app on it) reached out to a server elsewhere.
firewall.glossary.established: An active, currently open connection — data can flow both ways right now.
firewall.glossary.listen: This PC is waiting for incoming connections on this port, but nothing is connected yet.
firewall.glossary.time_wait: The connection just closed. Windows keeps it around briefly to make sure no leftover data arrives late.
firewall.glossary.close_wait: The other side closed the connection, and this PC is finishing cleanup on its end.
firewall.glossary.bound: A port reserved for use, but not actively listening or connected yet.
firewall.glossary.unverified: Not on a known-safe or known-bad list. Most everyday connections start out this way — it doesn't necessarily mean anything is wrong.
firewall.glossary.pid: Process ID — a temporary number Windows assigns to a running program while it is active.
firewall.glossary.whois: Looks up who owns/operates a given IP address, e.g. the company running that server.
firewall.perimeterSummaryText: {total} total connection{s} · {filtered} match current filters · {shown} shown on map
firewall.perimeterSummaryHidden: · {hidden} hidden by cap — raise "Show on map" to see more

audit.check.defender.name: Windows Defender
audit.check.rtp.name: Real-Time Protection
audit.check.uac.name: User Account Control (UAC)
audit.check.updates.name: Windows Updates
audit.check.bitlocker.name: BitLocker Drive Encryption
audit.check.execPolicy.name: PowerShell Execution Policy
audit.check.secureBoot.name: Secure Boot
audit.check.defender.enabled.msg: Defender antivirus is enabled and running.
audit.check.rtp.active.detail: Threats are blocked as they appear.
audit.check.rtp.off.detail: Your system is vulnerable to active threats.
audit.check.uac.disabled.msg: UAC is disabled! This is a severe security risk.
audit.check.uac.enabled.detail: UAC prompts before making system-level changes.
audit.check.uac.disabled.detail: All programs run with full administrator privileges.
audit.check.bitlocker.encrypted.detail: Your data is protected if the device is lost or stolen.
audit.check.bitlocker.notEncrypted.detai: Anyone with physical access can read your data.
audit.check.bitlocker.unknown.detail: Could not determine BitLocker protection status.
audit.check.bitlocker.unknown.msg: BitLocker status could not be determined.
audit.check.bitlocker.na.msg: BitLocker is not available on this system.
audit.check.bitlocker.na.detail: Requires Windows Pro/Enterprise and a TPM chip.
audit.check.execPolicy.secure.detail: Only signed or locally authored scripts can run.
audit.check.execPolicy.insecure.detail: Less restrictive execution policy may allow untrusted scripts.
audit.check.secureBoot.enabled.detail: Only trusted bootloaders can run during system startup.
audit.check.secureBoot.disabled.detail: System is vulnerable to bootkit attacks.
audit.check.defender.queryError.detail: The Get-MpComputerStatus cmdlet may not be available on this system.
audit.check.updates.parseError.detail: Unexpected response from Windows Update query.
audit.check.updates.queryError.detail: Windows Update may be disabled or the COM query timed out.
audit.check.bitlocker.info.msg: BitLocker status unavailable (may not be supported on this edition).
audit.check.bitlocker.info.detail: BitLocker requires Windows Pro or Enterprise.
audit.check.secureBoot.unknown.msg: Secure Boot status could not be determined.
audit.check.secureBoot.unknown.detail: This check may not be supported on virtual machines or older hardware.
audit.check.defender.rec: Keep Windows Update enabled for automatic definition updates.
audit.check.rtp.rec: Enable real-time protection in Windows Security settings.
audit.check.uac.rec: Enable UAC via Control Panel > User Accounts > Change User Account Control settings.
audit.check.updates.rec: Open Settings > Windows Update and install pending updates.
audit.check.bitlocker.rec: Enable BitLocker via Control Panel > BitLocker Drive Encryption.
audit.check.execPolicy.rec: Consider setting to RemoteSigned: Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope LocalMachine
audit.check.secureBoot.rec: Enable Secure Boot in your UEFI/BIOS firmware settings.
audit.check.execPolicy.rec2: Check execution policy with Get-ExecutionPolicy -List in PowerShell.
audit.check.bitlocker.rec2: Check BitLocker status in Windows settings.
